title: Next high-leverage phase: a guided field-season loop that makes the current systems feel like one game
summary: Twenty-second structured packet for Eco Explorer. The queue is clear because many foundational systems are now live: corridor travel, field requests, a field station, traversal geometry, sketchbook, sound, close-look inspect, and the living-world seam. The highest-value next phase is no longer another isolated feature. It is one short authored field-season loop that ties those systems together for a fresh-save player, followed by more authored content fuel, a second traversal-heavy biome, and only then a broader second-tier station or ecology-process expansion.

execution_order.goal: Beat-map the first guided field-season loop so the next main pass has a compact authored arc instead of another loose feature drop.
execution_order.why_now: The current systems are broad enough that the next leverage comes from coherence and pacing, not from adding one more disconnected subsystem.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first guided field-season loop for a fresh save.
execution_order.why_now: This is the shortest path from a strong systems sandbox to a stronger 'game' feeling.

execution_order.goal: Review the first guided field-season loop for cohesion, tone, and whether it actually makes the live systems feel connected.
execution_order.why_now: The first arc should be criticized before the project spends more content and traversal work feeding it.

execution_order.goal: Add a content fuel pack for the loop: more discoveries, stronger note/request support, and another comparison-worthy shared species pair.
execution_order.why_now: The live systems now risk outrunning the authored content that supports them.

execution_order.goal: Review the content fuel pack for science quality, pacing, and whether it makes the loop feel fuller rather than busier.
execution_order.why_now: More content is only leverage if it improves the actual player experience.

execution_order.goal: Spread traversal depth to a second biome after the forest proof.
execution_order.why_now: One traversal-heavy biome is a proof; two makes movement feel like a real pillar.

execution_order.goal: Add one second-tier field-station upgrade or utility unlock.
execution_order.why_now: A slightly richer station loop makes more sense after the first guided arc and a second traversal-heavy biome exist.

execution_order.goal: Wake up the next ecology-process layer as visible revisit change instead of generic ambience growth.
execution_order.why_now: Weather and phenology will land harder once the player has clearer authored reasons to revisit places.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
Do not respond to a clear queue with random polish work.
The next phase should emphasize cohesion over feature count.
The first guided loop should feel notebook-first and child-friendly, not like a quest-log tutorial funnel.
The field station should stay science-safe and observational rather than extraction-first.
Fresh-save onboarding should introduce live systems in a calm order instead of exposing everything immediately.
More content should feed the loop and the teaching, not just inflate totals.
Traversal spread should stay authored and forgiving, not precision-platform-heavy.
Any later ecology-process work should show pattern, shelter, timing, or visible habitat change, not just extra effects.
acceptance_focus: The next phase gives agents one cohesive direction instead of a blank queue.
The project starts feeling more like one authored game instead of a bag of successful subsystems.
Later content and process expansions now have a stronger player-loop foundation to land on.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-functional-gameplay-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-functional-gameplay-loop-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-next-high-leverage-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-world-continuity-preproduction-pack.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-corridor-stabilization-follow-up-review.md, and this packet. Treat the clear queue as a signal to start the next cohesion phase, not to drift into random polish. The highest-value next work is one short guided field-season loop, then more authored content fuel, then a second traversal-heavy biome, then a slightly richer station loop.
